source_pr.title: executor: add test case for memory cache in parition table
source_pr.body:
Signed-off-by: Shuaipeng Yu <jackysp@gmail.com>

<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #xxx <!-- REMOVE this line if no issue to close -->

Problem Summary:
Memory cache (manager) using table ID to manage cache for the read-only tables, which will not consider partitions (physical ID), but `lock table` also only using table ID. It's better to add some test cases to confirm the memory cache management still works well. Besides, the row ID/clustered index will use one allocator for the partition tables, it's better to confirm the
key will not be overwritten in the memory cache.

### What is changed and how it works?

Proposal: [xxx](url) <!-- REMOVE this line if not applicable -->

What's Changed:
Add test case for memory cache in the partition tables.

How it Works:

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- Unit test

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- No release note

symptoms: tk.MustQuery("select distinct(_tidb_rowid) from point order by _tidb_rowid").Check(testkit.Rows("1", "2")) (executor/point_get_test.go)
failure_signature: tk.MustQuery("select distinct(_tidb_rowid) from point order by _tidb_rowid").Check(testkit.Rows("1", "2")) (executor/point_get_test.go)
fix_pattern: tk.MustQuery("select distinct(_tidb_rowid) from point order by _tidb_rowid").Check(testkit.Rows("1", "2")) (executor/point_get_test.go)

evidence: evidence: tk.MustQuery("select distinct(_tidb_rowid) from point order by _tidb_rowid").Check(testkit.Rows("1", "2")) (executor/point_get_test.go)
